cases.label: Delayed broker acknowledgement stays active until proof arrives
Duplicate route blocked while prior lifecycle is open
Restart recovery attaches orphan broker position and keeps missing links visible
Recovered Capital position exits through close ack, absence proof, and P/L outcome
Recovered close acknowledgement stays active until broker absence proof
Alpaca, Binance, and Kraken open orders recover with broker ids
Partial fill keeps quantities, price, fees, and client/broker ids
Close acknowledgement does not become closed while broker still reports the position
Stale broker proof holds action and cannot certify lifecycle advancement
Recovered stale close proof cannot certify closed or outcome state
Broker failure and unknown states map to explicit lifecycle statuses

manual_boundaries: Binance order/test or Spot Testnet only

close_fill_proof: dealReference must be confirmed before dealId is trusted
requirements.recovery_expectation: On restart, recover Capital positions by dealId and attach them to a lifecycle.
requirements.requirement: Capital submit proof carries dealReference before broker acknowledgement.
requirements.timeout_behavior: Hold as submitted/unverified until confirm or positions proof resolves the reference.

requirements.recovery_expectation: Recovered Capital positions must retain dealId and route key.
requirements.requirement: Capital broker proof carries dealId after confirmation.
requirements.timeout_behavior: Do not promote to position_open until dealId or get_positions proof exists.

requirements.recovery_expectation: Startup reconciliation attaches orphan broker positions to recovered lifecycle ids.
requirements.requirement: Open exposure is verified from broker get_positions evidence.
requirements.timeout_behavior: If positions proof is stale or missing, keep lifecycle active but action-held.

close_fill_proof: position_closed requires position absence after close acknowledgement
requirements.recovery_expectation: Open dealIds survive restart until absence or closed-trade proof is recorded.
requirements.requirement: Close is only final after broker positions no longer include the deal.
requirements.timeout_behavior: A close acknowledgement without absence proof remains close_acknowledged or close_failed.

close_fill_proof: partial/full fills carry filled and remaining quantities
requirements.recovery_expectation: Startup query of open orders attaches client_order_id or broker order id.
requirements.requirement: Alpaca order status is correlated by client/order id and explicit lifecycle status.
requirements.timeout_behavior: Unknown REST result stays unverified until stream/query proof resolves it.

close_fill_proof: executionReport supplies order status, last fill, cumulative fill, and fees when available
requirements.recovery_expectation: Startup open-order query attaches newClientOrderId and orderId to lifecycle.
requirements.requirement: Binance newClientOrderId and executionReport/query proof reconcile order state.
requirements.source: Binance Spot API orders and user data stream
requirements.timeout_behavior: 5XX/timeout status is unknown until user stream or order query proof appears.

close_fill_proof: openOrders carries volume, filled volume, cost, fees, and status updates
requirements.recovery_expectation: Startup openOrders query attaches cl_ord_id and Kraken order id.
requirements.requirement: Kraken cl_ord_id/openOrders/order query proof reconciles order state.
requirements.source: Kraken Spot API cl_ord_id and openOrders
requirements.timeout_behavior: Unknown submit result stays unverified until openOrders or query proof resolves it.

close_fill_proof: active routes remain exclusive until terminal or recovered-closed proof
requirements.recovery_expectation: Recovered active routes block duplicate submissions after restart.
requirements.requirement: A second lifecycle is blocked while the same route is submitted/open.
requirements.timeout_behavior: Submitted/open/timeout-unverified lifecycles block duplicate route submission.

close_fill_proof: orphan state remains active until broker terminal proof appears
requirements.recovery_expectation: Recovered lifecycle ids are stable by broker deal/order id.
requirements.requirement: Broker state found on startup is attached to a recovered lifecycle with missing links visible.
requirements.timeout_behavior: Missing upstream links are visible as lifecycle_continuity_missing, not hidden.

close_fill_proof: close acknowledgement alone is not closed
requirements.recovery_expectation: Recovered routes stay duplicate-blocked until verified close or outcome proof.
requirements.requirement: A recovered Capital position can move through close request, close acknowledgement, broker absence, and final P/L outcome without fabricating upstream links.
requirements.timeout_behavior: Recovered exits remain active/attention until absence proof and P/L evidence are present.

close_fill_proof: open orders remain active until fill/cancel/expire/reject proof
requirements.recovery_expectation: Every recovered open order retains venue-specific client and broker ids.
requirements.requirement: Open Alpaca, Binance, and Kraken broker orders recover into active lifecycle rows.
requirements.timeout_behavior: Open-order recovery blocks duplicate route submission.

close_fill_proof: partial_fill stays active until full fill, cancel, expire, reject, or close proof
requirements.recovery_expectation: Recovered partial fills keep cumulative fill and remaining quantity.
requirements.requirement: Partial fills keep filled, remaining, average fill, fee, and broker id fields attached.
requirements.timeout_behavior: Partial fills are never treated as closed without terminal broker proof.

close_fill_proof: timeout status cannot imply rejection, fill, or close by itself
requirements.recovery_expectation: Startup query must resolve or preserve the unknown status visibly.
requirements.requirement: A timeout is held as unverified until broker query/stream proof resolves it.
requirements.timeout_behavior: Timeouts stay submit_timeout_unverified and block duplicate route submission.

close_fill_proof: stale proof can only hold/block, never certify position_closed or outcome_recorded
requirements.recovery_expectation: Recovered state must show stale proof as an explicit blocker.
requirements.requirement: Stale broker proof cannot advance order, fill, close, or P/L state.
status_sources: timestamped broker query or stream evidence
requirements.timeout_behavior: Stale proof remains order_blocked or close_failed until fresh broker proof arrives.

close_fill_proof: terminal order states cannot become open or closed without a new lifecycle
requirements.recovery_expectation: Startup recovery preserves terminal broker status and does not resubmit.
requirements.requirement: Rejected, cancelled, expired, failed, and rate-limited states map to explicit lifecycle events.
requirements.timeout_behavior: Rate limits and rejects are terminal/blocked evidence, not unknown success.

sandbox_manual_boundaries: Binance order/test or Spot Testnet only

sandbox_paper_cases.label: Capital demo GOLD sandbox/paper start-to-close chain
Alpaca paper status mapping and duplicate-route block
Binance test order keeps 5XX/timeout status unknown until verified
Kraken validate/openOrders recovery maps cl_ord_id, fills, fees, and status
Production broker order endpoints are rejected by sandbox/paper guards

sandbox_paper_requirements.requirement: Capital demo GOLD lifecycle carries demo-only dealReference, dealId, position presence, close acknowledgement, absence proof, and P/L.
Alpaca paper lifecycle is correlated by client_order_id or broker id and explicit order status/query or stream proof.
Binance sandbox proof uses /api/v3/order/test by default and treats 5XX/timeout as unknown until query or executionReport resolves it.
Kraken sandbox proof is validate-only placement plus openOrders/query recovery; no real Kraken order placement.
Production/live order endpoints are rejected before a sandbox_paper lifecycle can advance.

recovery_expectations: Recovered active routes block duplicate submissions after restart.
Recovered lifecycle ids are stable by broker deal/order id.
Every recovered open order retains venue-specific client and broker ids.
Recovered partial fills keep cumulative fill and remaining quantity.
Startup query must resolve or preserve the unknown status visibly.
Recovered state must show stale proof as an explicit blocker.
Startup recovery preserves terminal broker status and does not resubmit.
status_sources: timestamped broker query or stream evidence
timeout_behaviors: Submitted/open/timeout-unverified lifecycles block duplicate route submission.
Missing upstream links are visible as lifecycle_continuity_missing, not hidden.
Open-order recovery blocks duplicate route submission.
Partial fills are never treated as closed without terminal broker proof.
Timeouts stay submit_timeout_unverified and block duplicate route submission.
Stale proof remains order_blocked or close_failed until fresh broker proof arrives.
Rate limits and rejects are terminal/blocked evidence, not unknown success.

recovery_expectations: Startup query of open orders attaches client_order_id or broker order id.
timeout_behaviors: Unknown REST result stays unverified until stream/query proof resolves it.

recovery_expectations: Startup open-order query attaches newClientOrderId and orderId to lifecycle.
timeout_behaviors: 5XX/timeout status is unknown until user stream or order query proof appears.

recovery_expectations: On restart, recover Capital positions by dealId and attach them to a lifecycle.
Recovered Capital positions must retain dealId and route key.
Startup reconciliation attaches orphan broker positions to recovered lifecycle ids.
Open dealIds survive restart until absence or closed-trade proof is recorded.
Recovered routes stay duplicate-blocked until verified close or outcome proof.
timeout_behaviors: Hold as submitted/unverified until confirm or positions proof resolves the reference.
Do not promote to position_open until dealId or get_positions proof exists.
If positions proof is stale or missing, keep lifecycle active but action-held.
A close acknowledgement without absence proof remains close_acknowledged or close_failed.
Recovered exits remain active/attention until absence proof and P/L evidence are present.

recovery_expectations: Startup openOrders query attaches cl_ord_id and Kraken order id.
timeout_behaviors: Unknown submit result stays unverified until openOrders or query proof resolves it.
